slides.title: DOCSAM: Unified Document Image Segmentation via Query Decomposition and Heterogeneous Mixed Learning
slides.content:
Xiao-Hui Li¹, Fei Yin¹, Cheng-Lin Liu¹²
¹MAIS, Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing
²School of Artificial Intelligence, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
{xiaohui.li, fyin, liucl}@nlpr.ia.ac.cn

slides.title: Agenda
slides.content:
• Motivation: Why unified document image segmentation?
• Core Idea: Query decomposition + heterogeneous mixed learning
• Architecture Overview: Vision encoder, semantic/instance query interaction, hybrid decoder
• Key Innovations: Open-set classification via semantic queries, curriculum-aware training, instance query selection
• Comprehensive Evaluation: Multi-task, multi-dataset, ablation & SOTA comparison
• Conclusion & Impact

slides.title: The Challenge: Fragmented, Inefficient, Non-Adaptable Models
slides.content:
Document image segmentation (DIS) is foundational for text recognition, IE, and DocVQA — but remains fragmented:
• Task silos: Layout analysis, text detection, table recognition handled separately
• Dataset heterogeneity: 48+ datasets vary in document type (modern/ancient/handwritten/scene), language, annotation granularity (region/line/char/cell), and format
• Model limitations: Specialized architectures → redundant computation/storage; closed-set classifiers → poor generalization to new classes/tasks; static after training → no continual learning
→ Need a *unified*, *open-set*, *scalable*, and *adaptable* framework.

slides.title: DocSAM: A Unified Transformer Framework
slides.content:
DocSAM unifies diverse DIS tasks (layout analysis, multi-granularity text segmentation, table structure recognition) by modeling them as joint instance + semantic segmentation.
Key design principles:
• Query decomposition: Instance queries (learnable) + semantic queries (Sentence-BERT embedded class names)
• Cross-modal interaction: Semantic queries act as both natural-language prompts *and* class prototypes for instance classification
• Heterogeneous mixed learning: Trained end-to-end on 48 diverse datasets without task-specific heads or modifications

slides.title: Architecture Deep Dive: Hybrid Query Decoder (HQD)
slides.content:
Inside each HQD layer (K=4):
1. Concatenate & fuse Q_S (semantic) and Q_I (instance) via self-attention + FFN
2. Coarse-to-fine cross-attention with multi-scale image features (X_I) using two parallel Multi-Scale Decoders (L=4 scales)
3. Refine Q_S/Q_I via another self-attention + FFN
4. Predict masks, classes, and boxes:
   • Semantic mask: M_S = σ(Q_S × X_Mᵀ)
   • Instance mask: M_I = σ(Q_I × X_Mᵀ)
   • Instance class: Y_I = Softmax(Q_I × Q_Sᵀ) → open-set classification

slides.title: Training Strategy: Efficiency & Robustness
slides.content:
To enable scalable heterogeneous training:
• Curriculum learning: Group datasets by task/style; warm up with easier groups first
• Instance Query Selection (IQS): At layer k, keep only queries with class score > T_k = T_max / 2^(K−k), T_max=0.01 → reduces memory & speeds up training without performance loss
• Vision pre-training: Swin backbone pre-trained on 48 datasets via SimMIM (though ablation shows SimMIM harms performance — so frozen in final model)
• Sentence-BERT: Frozen during training (ablation confirms negligible impact on accuracy)

slides.title: Dataset Landscape: 48 Heterogeneous Benchmarks
slides.content:
DocSAM integrates the broadest benchmark coverage to date:
• Document Layout Analysis (10+ datasets): PubLayNet, DocLayNet, D4LA, M⁶Doc, SCUT-CAB, etc.
• Ancient & Handwritten Segmentation (8+): CASIA-AHCDB, SCUT-CAB-physical/logical, HJDataset, CASIA-HWDB, SCUT-HCCDoc
• Table Structure Recognition (12+): FinTabNet, PubTabNet, TableBank (latex/word), ICDAR cTDaR, NTable, WTW
• Scene Text Detection (18+): CTW1500, Total-Text, ICDAR2015/2017/2019/2023, MSRA-TD500, COCO-Text
All datasets listed in Table 1 — spanning languages (English, Chinese, Japanese, Bengali, multilingual), formats (PDF, scanned, camera-captured), and annotation types (pixel masks, bounding boxes, logical hierarchies).

slides.title: Ablation Study: What Makes DocSAM Work?
slides.content:
Ablation on 15 representative datasets (avg. metrics):
• Curriculum learning: +0.65% mAP, +1.83% mAF → accelerates convergence
• IQS: +4.49% mAP vs. w/o selection → validates efficiency gain
• Masked attention (critical): Removing it drops mAP by 8.14% → essential for refinement
• Query interaction: Removal drops mAP by 10.31% → proves semantic-instance synergy is core
• Freezing Sentence-BERT: No degradation (mAP: 0.2322 → 0.2322) → justifies freezing
• SimMIM pretraining: *Harms* performance (mAP drops from 0.3843 to 0.1002) → domain gap matters
• DocSAM-large: Best overall (mAP=0.3900, mIoU=0.6726)

slides.title: Unified Performance: Joint Pre-Training Results
slides.content:
DocSAM achieves strong zero-shot transfer across all 48 datasets *without fine-tuning*:
• Layout analysis: PubLayNet (mAP=0.806, mIoU=0.898), DocLayNet (mAP=0.484, mIoU=0.607)
• Handwritten docs: SCUT-HCCDoc (mAP=0.544, mIoU=0.844), CASIA-HWDB (mAP=0.721, mIoU=0.935)
• Tables: TableBank-latex (mAP=0.897, mIoU=0.951), PubTabNet (mAP=0.684, mIoU=0.860)
• Scene text: CTW1500 (mAP=0.400), Total-Text (mAP=0.405)
→ Consistent high performance across physical layout; lower on logical/layout requiring multimodal cues (e.g., D⁴LA, SCUT-CAB-logical) — expected for single-modal model.

slides.title: Transfer Learning Power: Fine-Tuning Gains
slides.content:
Fine-tuning DocSAM-large on individual datasets yields dramatic improvements over both scratch training and zero-shot inference:
• PubLayNet: mAP jumps from 0.830 → 0.854 (+2.4 pts); mIoU from 0.911 → 0.921
• SCUT-HCCDoc: mAP from 0.549 → 0.580 (+3.1 pts); mIoU from 0.847 → 0.862
• FinTabNet: mAP from 0.684 → 0.713 (+2.9 pts); mIoU from 0.860 → 0.870
• Unseen datasets: IIIT-AR-13K (mAP=0.638), CHDAC-2022 (mAP=0.687) — proves strong generalization
→ DocSAM serves both as a robust *versatile segmenter* and an effective *pre-trained foundation*.

slides.title: State-of-the-Art Comparison
slides.content:
DocSAM ranks among top performers across benchmarks:
• M⁶Doc: DocSAM mAP=0.663 — second only to DAT (0.712); outperforms TransDLANet (0.645), Deformable DETR (0.572)
• SCUT-CAB (Physical): DocSAM AP50=0.948 — best among all methods; matches SCNet (0.941) and exceeds Faster R-CNN (0.913)
• SCUT-CAB (Logical): DocSAM AP50=0.779 — competitive (vs. SCNet 0.836), limited by single-modality
• CTW1500/Total-Text: DocSAM F-score=0.842/0.770 — slightly behind top detectors (e.g., DAT-SEG: 0.917/0.920), but achieves this *with one unified model* across *all* tasks — not specialized per dataset.

slides.title: Qualitative Results: Versatility Across Domains
slides.content:
DocSAM delivers reliable segmentation across diverse real-world scenarios:
• Layout analysis (Fig. 4): Accurately segments text blocks, figures, tables in modern (PubLayNet), historical (D4LA), and multilingual (M⁶Doc) documents
• Handwritten docs (Fig. 5): Localizes lines/characters in complex ancient Chinese books (SCUT-CAB), Japanese scrolls (HJDataset), and unconstrained handwriting (SCUT-HCCDoc)
• Table recognition (Fig. 6): Detects cells in dense scientific tables (FinTabNet), LaTeX-generated tables (TableBank), and handwritten forms (cTDaR)
• Scene text (Fig. 7): Handles curved, multilingual, and low-resolution texts in street signs, posters, and storefronts
→ Demonstrates true cross-task, cross-domain generalization.

slides.title: Limitations & Failure Cases
slides.content:
Failure analysis (Fig. 8) reveals key challenges:
• Over-segmentation: Especially in large table cells containing many paragraphs/lines — often due to annotation ambiguity across datasets
• Imprecise bounding boxes: For dense, curved, or highly overlapping text lines and cells
• Scene text failures: Tiny, blurred, occluded, or extremely curved texts — where background complexity exceeds current visual capacity
• Logical layout gap: Lower performance on SCUT-CAB-logical, D⁴LA, M⁶Doc — highlights need for multimodal (text + layout + semantics) extension
→ These guide future work: better data augmentation, multimodal fusion, and geometric-aware modeling.

slides.title: Conclusion & Impact
slides.content:
• DocSAM is the first unified, transformer-based framework that handles *layout analysis*, *multi-granularity text segmentation*, and *table structure recognition* within a single architecture — eliminating task-specific models.
• By decomposing queries into instance + semantic and enabling heterogeneous mixed learning, it achieves open-set classification, robust generalization, and efficient continual learning.
• Validated on 48 datasets, it sets new standards for *accuracy*, *efficiency*, and *adaptability* — outperforming or matching SOTA across diverse benchmarks.
• Code released at https://github.com/xhli-git/DocSAM — enabling reproducibility and community advancement.
→ DocSAM represents a foundational step toward truly universal document understanding.
